headline: Iran Threatens Israel Over 84 Ceasefire Violations as Ukraine Strikes Deep Inside Russia
subheadline: Hezbollah launched rockets at Kfar Tebnit, IDF intercepted them, while Kyiv’s long‑range strike hit a Moscow‑region oil plant, expanding war depth.
lede: Iran’s Khatam al‑Anbiya headquarters warned Israel to stop 84 ceasefire violations in southern Lebanon or face a harsh response. Hezbollah fired a rocket salvo toward Israeli forces near Kfar Tebnit, which the IDF said it intercepted. Meanwhile, Ukrainian forces struck an oil‑processing plant 500 km from the front in the Moscow region, demonstrating deep‑strike capability. Russia’s air defence downed ten UAVs heading for Moscow, while both sides continue high‑tempo drone operations.

sections.title: Ukraine Extends Strikes Deep Into Russia
sections.body: Ukrainian long‑range missiles damaged an oil‑processing plant in the Moscow region, 500 km from the front. Russian air defence shot down ten UAVs bound for Moscow and claimed to have destroyed Ukrainian drone command points in Zaporizhzhia. The Ukrainian Ministry of Defence reported shooting down 172 enemy drones and listed numerous attacks across Russian territories. Civilian casualties rose as an FPV drone killed three people in Nikopol.

sections.title: Iran Warns Israel Amid Hezbollah Rocket Exchanges
sections.body: Iran’s Khatam al‑Anbiya headquarters warned Israel to halt 84 ceasefire violations in southern Lebanon or face a harsh response. Hezbollah announced a rocket salvo targeting Israeli forces near Kfar Tebnit, which the IDF said it intercepted. An Israeli air force unit struck a vehicle in southern Lebanon shortly after the rocket exchange. Diplomatic activity continues with a US‑Iran memorandum of understanding and US‑Qatar financial talks.

sections.title: Intelligence Assessment
sections.body: Both theatres show escalating low‑intensity exchanges that risk broader regional involvement, as Iran signals possible direct action and Ukraine pushes strike depth into Russian interior. The parallel increase in UAV activity and diplomatic maneuvering suggests heightened volatility if negotiations stall.
